page: Amazon Web Services c5a.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with multithreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

In benchmark testing, the c5a.large demonstrates mixed performance characteristics. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, along with Geekbench single-core tests, achieve average performance within the middle 50% of tested servers. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests indicate weak to poor performance, with small block read-only cached bandwidth in the bottom 10% and large block uncached read/write bandwidth in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the c5a.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, aligning with compute-optimized profiles. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which, combined with the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, may limit I/O-heavy workloads. The instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or high-throughput multi-core scaling, though users must trade off multi-threaded performance and memory bandwidth.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the c5a.large is suited for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, and small-scale utility applications. It is less appropriate for memory-intensive databases, large-scale multi-threaded compilation, or real-time high-throughput web serving due to its lower multi-core and memory bandwidth scores.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and a 3.3 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. Benchmarks reveal average single-core performance in gzip compression and Geekbench tests, contrasted by poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale utility tasks where a dedicated CPU allocation is required without a large memory footprint.

tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with AMD EPYC processing and DDR4 memory for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

4.0 GB of DDR4 system memory operating at 3 GHz
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in single-threaded compression and single-core benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
